secondary_archetype: Forward Deployed / Field PM with enterprise ownership

level_signals: 6+ years in product management, technical program management, or customer-facing product ownership
Base salary range $205,600–$257,000 USD consistent with senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years in product management, technical program management, or a customer-facing product ownership role.

Demonstrated record of shipping products or platform capabilities into large organizations — not pilots or proofs-of-concept. You can name the customer, the scale, the integration, and what production actually looked like.
Extreme ownership and follow-through: closes loops without reminders, drives outcomes across teams without formal authority, holds self and others to commitments.
Comfortable operating with senior customer stakeholders (VP/C-level) and senior engineers simultaneously; credible presence in high-stakes rooms on both sides.
Strong judgment under pressure: makes good calls quickly with incomplete information, knows when to escalate vs. resolve, does not generate thrash.
Technical fluency sufficient to hold a real conversation with a platform engineer about architectural tradeoffs — not deep coding, but genuine comprehension of how systems work in production.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Direct experience with AI/ML platform products — data labeling, RLHF, fine-tuning workflows, or model evaluation pipelines.
Experience in a startup or early-stage environment where process didn't exist and had to be created.
Prior forward-deployed or embedded role (consulting, field PM, solutions engineering with ownership).

explicitly_excluded: Pilots or proofs-of-concept (as substitutes for production deployments)

scope.surface: Enterprise customer deployments of Scale AI platform — embedded in customer accounts, driving from contract to production
primary_metrics_inferred: Quality and specificity of product signal fed back to platform team
core_responsibilities: Own product outcomes for our highest impact enterprise accounts. You are the accountable PM for product delivery and customer value.
Build trusted advisory relationships with VP/C-level buyers and senior technical leads.
Drive deployments from contract to production.
Translate real customer workflows, friction, and priorities into high-signal product input for core platform teams.
Distinguish where the product is the constraint from where execution, integration, or change management is.
Partner closely with FDEs so the account runs as a coordinated team, not a set of parallel workstreams.
Identify and develop expansion opportunities grounded in demonstrated customer value
Represent customer reality to platform and leadership with enough credibility that it changes decisions.

compensation.equity_form: equity grant (subject to Board of Director approval)

green_flags: Role scope is unusually specific and clearly differentiated from CSM/SE roles
Equity mentioned as part of compensation package
Explicit 90-day reconsideration policy shows structured hiring process
Named enterprise customers (Meta, Cisco, Mayo Clinic, U.S. Army/Air Force) signal real deployment scale
red_flags: Location type not explicitly stated in JD body as hybrid vs. onsite — inferred as onsite from pay transparency listing specific cities only
Role is extremely demanding — 'extreme ownership', multi-quarter engagements with VP/C-level, and production deployment accountability may imply high-pressure environment
No clarity on portfolio size or number of accounts the FDPM would own simultaneously

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + 6+ years + stakeholder expectations)
location.type (inferred as onsite from pay transparency listing SF/NY/Seattle; body does not specify hybrid policy)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer examples)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
ambiguities: Location type: JD body does not state hybrid vs. onsite. Pay transparency section names SF, New York, Seattle as pay locations, suggesting those are the expected work locations, but does not confirm in-office requirement. Treated as onsite pending confirmation.
